@@ -1324,7 +1324,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","title":"Implement: terminal grid data model (cells, lines, cursor, modes)","description":"Goal
- Implement the core in-memory model used by the terminal engine.

Requirements
- Cell model: text (grapheme), fg/bg, attrs (bold/italic/underline/etc), hyperlink id, and width.
- Grid model: fixed columns, rows; supports efficient row clears and spans.
- Cursor + modes: wrap, origin, insert, autowrap; plus placeholders for DEC modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for all basic mutations.
- No panics on edge cases (min size, zero-length operations).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:58:22.262569874Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:58:22.262549566Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","title":"Implement: scrollback + resize/reflow policy","description":"Goal
- Support realistic scrollback and correct behavior under resize.

Requirements
- Scrollback buffer with configurable capacity.
- Clear reflow policy: what happens to wrapped lines on resize.
- Resize invariants: cursor position, viewport, selection (if any), and mode semantics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic replay fixtures for resize storms.
- Bench coverage for resize cost and memory.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:03:09.224829755Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:03:09.224807123Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","title":"Implement: selection model + copy extraction","description":"Goal
- Implement selection primitives (char/cell/word/line) and copy extraction from grid+scrollback.

Requirements
- Coordinate model for selections spanning scrollback and viewport.
- Word selection heuristics tuned for code and paths.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests for selection across wrapped lines and resize.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:49.789352547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:43:45.355097628Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:43:45.355078763Z","close_reason":"Implemented selection model + copy extraction (Selection/BufferPos) with wrapped+resize tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:49.789352547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:49.789352547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","title":"Implement: VT/ANSI parser state machine (CSI/OSC/SGR + edits)","description":"Goal
- Implement the output stream parser and apply mutations to the grid.

Must Support (initial)
- CSI cursor moves, erase in line/screen, insert/delete char/line, scroll up/down, set scroll region.
- SGR attributes and colors (16/256/truecolor).
- OSC 0/2 (title) (optional storage) and OSC 8 hyperlinks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance fixtures from bd-lff4p.1.1 replay to exact expected grid snapshots.
- Proptest: parser never panics on arbitrary byte streams.

+{"id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","title":"Implement: VT/ANSI parser state machine (CSI/OSC/SGR + edits)","description":"Goal
- Implement the output stream parser and apply mutations to the grid.

Must Support (initial)
- CSI cursor moves, erase in line/screen, insert/delete char/line, scroll up/down, set scroll region.
- SGR attributes and colors (16/256/truecolor).
- OSC 0/2 (title) (optional storage) and OSC 8 hyperlinks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance fixtures from bd-lff4p.1.1 replay to exact expected grid snapshots.
- Proptest: parser never panics on arbitrary byte streams.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.7","title":"Implement: hyperlink registry (OSC 8) + click mapping","description":"Goal
- Track OSC 8 hyperlinks in a compact registry and expose hit-testing hooks for the web renderer.

Acceptance Criteria
- Fixtures cover overlapping hyperlinks, resets, and clear-on-scroll behavior.
- API supports mapping cell->url efficiently.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:02.076522360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:42:33.837962633Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:42:33.837944259Z","close_reason":"Implemented HyperlinkRegistry + click mapping; Scrollback eviction hook + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:02.076522360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:17:57.102713817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:17:57.400830719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","title":"Implement: incremental patch API for renderer (dirty spans, diff runs)","description":"Goal
- Provide an efficient change representation so renderers can update incrementally.

Requirements
- Dirty tracking at row + span granularity.
- Stable, deterministic patch ordering.
- APIs designed for both WebGPU and native present paths.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchmarks demonstrate sub-linear updates for typical workloads.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:07.608920515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:10:40.965383507Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:10:40.965364181Z","close_reason":"Completed: DirtyTracker + GridDiff + ChangeRun + enhanced Patch (132 tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:07.608920515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:07.608920515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:07.608920515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","title":"Test: proptest invariants for grid + parser (never panic; stable semantics)","description":"Goal
- Add property tests that make it safe to aggressively optimize and refactor.

Invariants (examples)
- Cursor always within bounds.
- Lines never exceed width in cell count.
- Parser accepts arbitrary bytes without panic.
- Applying a patch preserves semantic equality vs full recompute.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run in CI with fixed seeds + shrinkable counterexamples.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1352,7 +1352,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","title":"Implement: new crate frankenterm-web (wasm-bindgen API + build plumbing)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/frankenterm-web` with WASM bindings and a minimal JS-facing API.

Requirements
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Expose: init(canvas_id or handle), resize, feed_input, present_patch.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:46.389546730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:05:16.886949555Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:05:16.886926582Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:46.389546730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:46.389546730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:46.389546730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","title":"Optional: auto URL detection (non-OSC8) + safe click handling","description":"Goal
- Detect plain-text URLs in the viewport/scrollback and make them clickable (similar to xterm.js WebLinks addon).

Scope
- Heuristics tuned to avoid false positives in code.
- Security: sanitize and enforce allowed protocols.

Testing
- Fixture corpus for common URL forms.
- E2E: click opens expected target (or emits link event) with JSONL logs.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","title":"Implement: WebGPU renderer skeleton (device init + background cell quads)","description":"Goal
- Get pixels on screen: render the terminal background + cell backgrounds at interactive speeds.

Requirements
- WebGPU init (adapter/device/queue) + resize handling.
- Draw pass for background and per-cell bg colors.
- Path for applying dirty-span patches only (no full redraw required).

Acceptance Criteria
- A minimal web harness can render a synthetic grid and resize without artifacts.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseStream","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:45:26.461656580Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","title":"Implement: glyph rasterization + atlas cache (monospace first)","description":"Goal
- Turn glyphs into atlas textures with stable metrics and minimal overhead.

Requirements
- Start with monospace ASCII + box drawing + common symbols; expand to full Unicode coverage via bd-lff4p.2.18.
- Deterministic glyph keys/ids across runs and platforms (within a given font build).
- Cache with explicit eviction policy + metrics (hit/miss, bytes, upload cost).
- Hot path: no allocations when glyph already cached.

Testing
- Unit tests for key stability and eviction invariants.
- Golden fixtures for a representative glyph set (metrics + atlas coordinates).
- Bench harness integration to measure cache miss cost and steady-state throughput.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchable API: given a glyph key, returns atlas coords and advances with no allocations in hot path.
- Upload + packing strategy is measurable and regression-gated.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:51.394478179Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","title":"Implement: glyph rasterization + atlas cache (monospace first)","description":"Goal
- Turn glyphs into atlas textures with stable metrics and minimal overhead.

Requirements
- Start with monospace ASCII + box drawing + common symbols; expand to full Unicode coverage via bd-lff4p.2.18.
- Deterministic glyph keys/ids across runs and platforms (within a given font build).
- Cache with explicit eviction policy + metrics (hit/miss, bytes, upload cost).
- Hot path: no allocations when glyph already cached.

Testing
- Unit tests for key stability and eviction invariants.
- Golden fixtures for a representative glyph set (metrics + atlas coordinates).
- Bench harness integration to measure cache miss cost and steady-state throughput.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchable API: given a glyph key, returns atlas coords and advances with no allocations in hot path.
- Upload + packing strategy is measurable and regression-gated.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldForest","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:09:31.782856563Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","title":"Implement: WebGPU text rendering (atlas + instanced quads)","description":"Goal
- Render terminal text cells using the glyph atlas and an instanced quad pipeline.

Requirements
- Respect per-cell fg color + attrs (bold/italic/underline as feasible).
- Handle wide glyphs and combining marks (at least gracefully).

Acceptance Criteria
- 120x40 grid @ 60fps with typical demo workloads on modern desktop.
- Instrumented frame timing (CPU submit + GPU time if possible).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","title":"Implement: cursor + selection + hyperlink hover/click rendering","description":"Goal
- Make the terminal feel interactive: cursor, selection visualization, and hyperlink affordances.

Requirements
- Cursor rendering styles: block, bar, underline.
- Selection rendering + copy.
- Hyperlink hover underline + click dispatch.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic visual tests (golden images or checksumable framebuffer dumps).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","title":"Implement: web input capture layer (keyboard/mouse/touch)","description":"Goal
- Capture browser input events and feed them into the ftui/FrankenTerm world with minimal latency and deterministic semantics.

Requirements
- Keyboard
  - consistent modifier semantics across browsers/OS
  - repeat handling
  - special keys and function keys
  - composition events integrate with IME plan (bd-lff4p.2.9)
- Mouse
  - click/drag/wheel with correct cell coordinates (respects DPR + font metrics)
  - selection gestures
- Touch
  - tap, long-press, scroll; optional pinch zoom

Testing + Logging
- Unit tests for key mapping tables and edge cases (Ctrl/Alt/Meta/Super).
- Property tests for modifier state machines (no stuck modifiers; idempotent focus loss).
- E2E scripts emit JSONL: event counts, encoded bytes (when relevant), and any mapping fallbacks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic event encoding suitable for record/replay.
- No key ghosting; no stuck modifiers; focus changes handled correctly.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","title":"Refactor: runtime backend abstraction (no direct Crossterm usage)","description":"Goal
- Introduce a backend abstraction so ftui-runtime can run on:
  - native tty backend (custom)
  - wasm backend (JS-driven)

Acceptance Criteria
- `Program` no longer owns a concrete `TerminalSession` tied to Crossterm.
- Backends provide: size, input events (or bytes), present output (or patches), and mode toggles.
- Old code paths removed after migration (no lingering shims).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","title":"Implement: WASM step-based Program runner (no blocking poll, no threads)","description":"Goal
- Implement a deterministic, JS-driven runtime loop for WASM.

Requirements
- Inputs are delivered from JS (keyboard/mouse/resize/ticks).
- Rendering produces either ftui-render Buffer diffs or FrankenTerm patches.
- No `std::thread` assumptions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can drive an ftui Model with: init -> input -> view -> present cycles.
- Deterministic record/replay possible.

- Connect ftui-render output to FrankenTerm so the browser renderer can present ftui frames efficiently.

Options
- Convert ftui-render Buffer diffs into FrankenTerm patch representation.
- Or define a shared patch protocol used by both.

Acceptance Criteria
- No full-frame copies on steady-state updates.
- End-to-end demo updates are visibly smooth.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","title":"Demo: run ftui-demo-showcase in browser via ftui-web + FrankenTerm","description":"Goal
- Run `ftui-demo-showcase` fully client-side in the browser via `ftui-web` + `frankenterm-web` (no xterm.js).

Scope
- Boot, render, navigate, resize, and respond to keyboard + mouse with low latency.
- Any OS-dependent features must either:
  - have explicit web implementations, OR
  - be explicitly disabled with user-visible UX (no silent partial failure).
- Perf + stability: no leaks, no jank, and predictable frame-time under typical demo workloads.

Testing + Logging (Non-negotiable)
- Create a browser "demo sweep" analogous to `tests/e2e/scripts/test_demo_showcase_sweep.sh`:
  - emits detailed JSONL (screen_id, seed, size, DPR, timings, frame/patch hashes)
  - failures emit minimal repro traces that can be replayed
- Determinism: recorded sessions replay to identical hashes on the same build.

Acceptance Criteria
- A single web page can run the demo for 10+ minutes without runaway memory or degraded responsiveness.
- JSONL logs validate against the shared schema and are rich enough to debug mismatches.
- Seed/time-freeze determinism is preserved end-to-end.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","title":"Implement: Unix input reader (poll + bytes) feeding ftui-core InputParser","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm event polling with a tight byte-reader + ftui-core parsing.

Requirements
- Non-blocking or poll-based read with timeout.
- Convert bytes to ftui-core Event via existing InputParser.
- Support resize, focus, bracketed paste, mouse sequences.

Acceptance Criteria
- Input parity tests vs current behavior for core keys and mouse sequences.
- No busy-looping when idle.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","title":"Implement: resize detection + size queries (SIGWINCH)","description":"Goal
- Provide accurate, low-latency resize handling without Crossterm.

Requirements
- Query terminal size via ioctl.
- Deliver resize events via SIGWINCH or equivalent.

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress test: resize storm does not drop or reorder events; no panics.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","title":"Integrate: swap ftui runtime to new backend (native path)","description":"Goal
- Wire the new native backend into `ftui-runtime` and remove all direct Crossterm coupling from the runtime hot path.

Non-Negotiable Compatibility
- Behavior must match current demo semantics (input, resize, mouse, paste, focus, kitty keyboard, alt-screen/inline rules).
- One-writer rule and terminal cleanup semantics must remain correct under normal exit and panic/unwind.

Testing + Logging
- Run the existing PTY E2E suite (`tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` or curated subsets) against the new backend.
- E2E logs must validate against `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` and include backend identity + capability profile.
- Add/extend tests as needed to cover any behavior differences discovered during migration.

Acceptance Criteria
- `ftui-demo-showcase` runs on the new backend with identical user-visible behavior.
- E2E suite is green and produces detailed JSONL logs usable for regression triage.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.8","title":"Optimize: native IO (syscall minimization, buffering, writev)","description":"Goal
- After Crossterm removal, push native IO performance beyond prior baselines.

Acceptance Criteria
- Measured present-time reductions (p95) in demo showcase.
- Changes follow Extreme Optimization loop (one lever, isomorphism proof).
